reasoning: Unlimited or wildly oversized approvals let the spender drain the wallet at any future time, not just at the moment of signing.
evidence_source: Well-documented EVM approval-exploit pattern, applicable to any chain running ERC-20/BEP-20 tokens.

reasoning: Approvals to brand-new, unverified contracts are the single most common vector in phishing-drain scams.
evidence_source: Well-documented EVM phishing pattern, applicable to any chain.

reasoning: A token that can be bought but not sold (via blacklist, tax trap, or blocked transfer logic) is the defining signature of a honeypot.
evidence_source: Standard honeypot-detection heuristic used across chain-agnostic scam-token scanners.

reasoning: Liquidity added and then rapidly withdrawn is the classic rug-pull sequence: attract buyers, then pull the exit liquidity.
evidence_source: Well-documented DeFi rug-pull pattern, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: A deployer holding a large share of supply with no liquidity lock retains the ability to dump on holders at will.
evidence_source: Standard tokenomics red flag used by chain-agnostic scam-token scanners.

reasoning: Near-identical names/symbols to an established token (single-character swaps, homoglyphs) are a standard impersonation scam.
evidence_source: Well-documented typosquatting pattern in token scams, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: Contract control moving to a freshly created wallet shortly before other suspicious activity often precedes an exploit or exit scam.
evidence_source: Standard governance red flag, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: A self-destruct call sending remaining balance to an address other than the documented treasury is a hard drain signal.
evidence_source: EVM-level primitive behavior, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: Upgrading a proxy to an unverified implementation can silently change all contract logic users previously trusted.
evidence_source: Well-documented proxy-pattern risk (OpenZeppelin upgradeable contracts), chain-agnostic.

reasoning: Borrowing and repaying within one block while an on-chain price oracle swings sharply is the standard flash-loan price-manipulation signature.
evidence_source: Well-documented DeFi attack pattern (e.g. bZx, Harvest Finance style exploits), chain-agnostic.

reasoning: A trade bracketed by two transactions from the same address in one block is the classic sandwich-attack pattern.
evidence_source: Well-documented MEV pattern, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: External calls made before internal balance/state updates are the root cause of classic reentrancy exploits (e.g. the original DAO hack pattern).
evidence_source: Foundational, chain-agnostic smart-contract vulnerability class.

reasoning: Delegatecall to an address controlled by the transaction sender can let an attacker execute arbitrary code in the calling contract's storage context.
evidence_source: Well-documented EVM vulnerability class (e.g. Parity multisig freeze), chain-agnostic.

reasoning: Off-chain signature requests disguised as 'claim' or 'airdrop' flows are a leading wallet-drain vector; new domains requesting broad permit/approval signatures are high risk.
evidence_source: Well-documented wallet-drainer phishing pattern, chain-agnostic.

reasoning: Coordinated wallets funded from one source trading back and forth inflates apparent volume without real economic activity.
evidence_source: Standard wash-trading detection heuristic; also explicitly the kind of programmatic small-trade visibility-boosting described in CiaoTool's own public documentation (ciaotool.gitbook.io).

reasoning: Multiple wallets buying in the deployment block, funded from one source, indicates coordinated insider sniping rather than organic demand.
evidence_source: Standard sniping/bundling detection heuristic; also explicitly a described feature category ('bundling') in CiaoTool's own public documentation.

reasoning: Rapid, large withdrawals from a bridge relative to its total locked value is the pattern seen in several major real-world bridge exploits.
evidence_source: Modeled on publicly reported cross-chain bridge exploits including Ronin Bridge (Mar 2022, compromised validator keys) and Poly Network (Aug 2021, contract logic flaw) — approximate figures, widely reported at the time, not BOT Chain-specific incidents.

reasoning: Minting wrapped assets on a destination chain without matching locked collateral on the source chain is a bridge-integrity failure mode.
evidence_source: Modeled on the publicly reported Wormhole bridge exploit (Feb 2022, signature verification bypass) — widely reported at the time, not a BOT Chain-specific incident.

reasoning: Flash-loan-funded manipulation of a lending protocol's collateral/liquidation accounting is a recurring exploit class in DeFi lending.
evidence_source: Modeled on the publicly reported Euler Finance exploit (Mar 2023, flawed donation/liquidation logic) — widely reported at the time, not a BOT Chain-specific incident.
